title: Long-form Journalism Study
description: Reading investigations of 10,000+ words as a craft discipline.
summary_pt_br: Jornalismo longo: leia o lead 3x, marque viradas, rastreie fontes, resuma em 200, critique em 200.

scenes.subtitle_en: Reading investigations of 10,000+ words as a craft discipline.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Jornalismo longo: leia o lead 3x, marque viradas, rastreie fontes, resuma em 200, critique em 200.

scenes.text_en: Reading investigations of 10,000+ words as a craft discipline. Focus: long-form journalism study. The lede does a decade of work in eighty words.
scenes.text_pt: Jornalismo longo: leia o lead 3x, marque viradas, rastreie fontes, resuma em 200, critique em 200. Foco: long-form journalism study. O lead faz uma década de trabalho em 80 palavras.

scenes.title: Long-form Journalism Study
scenes.body_en: Long-form method: read the lede three times. Mark each narrative turn. Track the reporter's sources. Write a 200-word summary. Then a 200-word critique.
scenes.body_pt: Jornalismo longo: leia o lead 3x, marque viradas, rastreie fontes, resuma em 200, critique em 200.

examples.en: The lede does a decade of work in eighty words.
examples.pt: O lead faz uma década de trabalho em 80 palavras.

examples.en: Count the sources — investigative pieces rest on twenty, not two.
examples.pt: Conte as fontes — investigações têm 20, não 2.

examples.en: Summary first, then critique. Never reverse the order.
examples.pt: Resumo antes da crítica. Nunca inverta.

exercise.question: Complete: "The lede does a ___ of work in eighty words."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "The lede does a decade of work in eighty words."
exercise.hint_pt_br: O lead faz uma década de trabalho em 80 palavras.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: Count the sources — investigative pieces rest on twenty, not two.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: Correct: "Count the sources — investigative pieces rest on twenty, not two."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Conte as fontes — investigações têm 20, não 2.

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Long-form Journalism Study — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre long-form journalism study. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Kumar — start a long-form read. What's first?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Read the lede three times in a row.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Highlight every named source in margin.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Two hundred words, no critique yet.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Only after the summary is written.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Long-form teaches you to read journalism as craft.

scenes.title: The investigation
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe the five-step long-form reading method. Use at least three journalism terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In his Saturday advanced reading class, Kumar handed each student the same twelve-thousand-word investigation.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: The lede does a decade of work in eighty words — read it three times before you read anything else in the piece.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Then mark each narrative turn in the margin — the points where the reporter shifts scene or timeline?

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Exactly. Count the named sources as you read — investigative pieces rest on twenty sources, never two or three.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Two-hundred-word summary first, then a two-hundred-word critique — you never reverse the order of that discipline.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Long-form journalism, Kumar insisted, teaches you to read journalism as craft rather than simply as information.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Long-form Journalism Study". Reading investigations of 10,000+ words as a craft discipline. The lede does a decade of work in eighty words. Count the sources — investigative pieces rest on twenty, not two. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Long-form Journalism Study". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
check.explanation: She says the title: "Long-form Journalism Study".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Long-form Journalism Study".

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.

sources.label: TED Talks · Work and Leadership
